title: Injection water pre-treatment and injection system and method
abstract: A system and a method for groundwater pre-treatment and injection are disclosed. The pre-treatment and injection system comprises subsea water wells (1, 1') connectable to a water injection system (2) comprising a water injection pump (3) in flow communication with a water injection well (4), wherein the subsea water wells (1, 1') are made as a hole into a groundwater holding strata under the mud-line (ML) with a water permeable (9') or non-permeable (9) completion using the seafloor soil for filtration, with an inner tubing (12, 12') connectable to the seawater injection system (2) via a valve (5).
claims:
Claims 1. A system for seawater pre-treatment and injection comprising at least one subsea water well (1, 1 ') connectable to a seawater injection system (2) comprising at least a water injection pump (3) in flow communication with a water injection well (4), wherein the subsea water well (1, ) is made as a hole into a groundwater holding strata under the mud- line (ML) with a water permeable (9') or non-permeable (9) completion using the seafloor soil for filtration, with an inner tube (12, 12') connectable to the seawater injection system (2) via a valve (5). 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the subsea water well (1) comprises an open hole completion (9) into which groundwater rises via the open end of the completion. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the subsea water well ( ) comprises a cased hole completion (9') into which groundwater penetrates via a permeable or perforated (13) completion wall. 4. The system of any previous claim, wherein a filter (14, 15) is installed internally in the subsea water well. 5. The system of claim 4, comprising a vertically layered multimedia filter (15) covering an annulus formed between a permeable completion wall (9') and a perforated inner tube (12') that extends through the filter. 6. The system of claim 4 or 5, wherein above the annulus filter (15) a portion of the completion (9') is filled by a solid plug. 7. The system of any previous claim, wherein a set of subsea water wells (1, ) are connectable to the seawater injection system (2) via a manifold and a water feed line (6). 8. The system of any previous claim, comprising a supplementary filter (7) or membrane (21) installed in the water feed line between the subsea water well/wells (1, 1 ') and the seawater injection system (2). 9. The system of claim 8, wherein the supplementary filter or membrane includes fine filtration units in the form of at least one of an ultra- filtration unit (14), a nano- filtration unit (15) for sulphates removal and a reverse osmosis unit (16) for desalination. 10. The system of claim 7, wherein a pump (20) is arranged in the water supply line between the subsea water well (1) and the supplementary filter (7) or membrane (21). 11. The system of any previous claim, wherein the subsea water well (1, ) is incorporated as part of a driven pile, a mud mat, a gravity anchor or a suction anchor. 12. A method for seawater pre-treatment and injection comprises extraction of seawater from at least one subsea water well (SWW) that uses the seafloor soil for filtration, and feeding the extracted water to a water injection pump in a seawater injection system (SWI) in flow communication with a water injection well. 13. The method of claim 12 comprising: - forming a bore in the seafloor soil, - inserting a permeable or non-permeable completion (9, 9') in the bore, - arranging an inner tube (12, 12') within the completion (9, 9'), and - connecting (5) the inner tube to a water injection pump (3). 14. The method of claim 13 comprising: - passing groundwater through a multimedia filter (15) which is arranged about a perforated length of the inner tube (12') inside a permeable well completion (9'). 15. The method of any of claims 12-13, comprising: - treating seawater in fine filtration units (7, 21) arranged between the subsea water well (1, 1 ') and the water injection pump (3). 16. The method of claim 15, comprising the following steps: 

 - supplementary treatment of seawater through at least one of ultra-filtration, nano- filtration, sulphates removal, desalination and C0 2 removal. 17. Use of seawater treated according to any of claims 12-16 for hydrocarbon recovery.
